question: [File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: estimateFees()] Can an unprivileged external user set highVolumeThrottle=10000 in repeated POST /api/v1/network/fees requests to force the HighVolumePricingCalculator to return artificially inflated fee multipliers, causing all client-submitted transactions to be priced out and effectively freezing network transaction throughput for the duration of the block?
